- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F12229202&title=18.%20Why%20Guys%20Who%20Take%20Sexy%20Photos%20Of%20Girls%20Are%20Fucked%20%7C%20The%20Mystery&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=0166e32d-cc6c-4a1c-b85e-97ed6eff4d1c0dcfe4&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    18\. Why Guys Who Take Sexy Photos Of Girls Are Fucked 

When I started taking pictures... Here is what I brought to the table: 

**1\. Angles**

 _I studied pin-up photography in such depth and eventually mastered "how to pose a girl"._

**2\. Rawness**

 _Taking party photos made me figure out how to make the "raw and dirty"... Look beautiful._

**3\. Art**

 _I could always take a better picture of a girl than she could take of herself._

But fast-forward to today.

None of those things matter anymore.  

We live in the age of Instagram.

The age of selfies.

When a girl takes photos of herself they're often just as good (insofar as emotional communication) as the ones she took with a "professional" photographer. She knows her angles better than you. 

Her selfies are way more authentic, more raw, more real, and more natural than anything that YOU could ever take. Why? She knows the model better! 😂

Her selfies capture people's attention and imagination faster. They project a mysterious energy that your "planned studio photoshoots" just can't match.

**The dinosaur "sexy photos" photographer is now competing against the model's "all access" "sexy photos" of HERSELF!  **

So the question is..

Why SHOULD she make plans, drive there, suffer introductions, worry about her safety (or you hitting on her non-stop) and then have to wait 3 months to get photos back?

_No, seriously. Why?_

What are you bringing to the table that she can't do on her own already? 

And in the comfort of her home, no less. With her cute new phone and her bestie acting as a human tripod.

_You can ignore these questions and become extinct_

Or you can re-align your creativity to your true power.

**1\. Make "human connection" part of your art.**

**2\. Find your message so that it's not just photos but an 'art project'.**

**3\. Make your photos about the stories too. Elevate their context.  **

_Those are just SOME ideas. Do one. Do them all (like lastnightsparty). Or find your own._

Not only will you be hearing the word "yes" more when you ask someone to shoot... But people will come out of the woodwork now, seeking YOU out. That's what happens when you discover and nurture the "thing" you possess that only you can offer.

But if you don't have your answers yet. I got you.

I suggest you listen closely to the girls in this section. These muses are still investing quality time with photographers... Even though they don't have to anymore.

So get into full-on "empathy mode" _for these 'FEMINISM FOR BOYS' modules._

And take a listen to what these girls have to say...

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

I disagree with what she said, that thought was being tested three years ago when people thought they could take photos with Iphones. This year in 2024 they are all coming back wanting professional ideas and equipment again. When photographers and models work together and build really good re pore and trust then magic happens. Collaboration is still beneficial and important in having success with photography.

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12229202/comments/14723808)

I just never knew the story we bring to the art was so important. I always knew mine behind it but I figured I wanted people to decipher the art for themselves. People have been thanking me now that the story of authenticity is involved. It's a nice side effect to see so many people seeking me out with deposit money ready all of a sudden.

Elyzabeth Skapik

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12229202/comments/13403120)

This speaks to me. I have never resonated with the influencer thing, I can feel when someone is just being “wallpaper.” I love creating from a deeper place where people can feel and connect with the expression. I’ve had more of a blog style, but I like the concept of treating it like an art project. It gives a sort of permission to put more thought into the expression rather than just capturing a moment in life.
